contents.value: **IMPORT** — ECL keyword.
**EXPORT** — ECL keyword.
**MODULE** — ECL keyword.
**END** — ECL keyword.
**RECORD** — ECL keyword.
**OUTPUT** — Built-in function/command in ECL.
**COUNT** — Built-in function/command in ECL.
**SUM** — Built-in function/command in ECL.
**MAX** — Built-in function/command in ECL.
**MIN** — Built-in function/command in ECL.
A **SET** is an unordered collection of unique values.
A **DATASET** represents a collection of records.
**TRANSFORM** applies a function to dataset records.
**SORT** orders records by specified fields.
**JOIN** combines records from two datasets on a key.
**FILTER** selects records matching a condition.
**GROUP** aggregates records by key fields.
**PROJECT** selects specific fields from records.
A **FUNCTION** defines a reusable computation.
A **MACRO** generates code from a template.
An **ATTRIBUTE** is a named field within a record.

```ecl
STRING[n]
```
A fixed-length or variable-length character string data type in ECL. STRING without a size specifier is variable-length. STRING[n] declares a fixed-length string of n characters. Strings support concatenation (+), comparison, and pattern matching operations for text processing.

```ecl
INTEGER[n]
```
A signed integer data type in ECL. The optional size specifier [n] indicates the number of bytes (1-8) used for storage, determining the range of values. INTEGER4 is a 4-byte signed integer (-2^31 to 2^31-1). Without a size, defaults to INTEGER8 for maximum range.

```ecl
REAL[n]
```
A floating-point numeric data type in ECL using IEEE 754 representation. REAL4 provides single-precision (32-bit) and REAL8 provides double-precision (64-bit) floating-point numbers. Used for scientific computations and values requiring decimal precision.

```ecl
ROLLUP(dataset, condition, transform)
```
Combines adjacent records that match the specified condition into a single record using the transform function. The dataset must be sorted by the grouping fields first. ROLLUP is used for aggregation, deduplication, and summarization of sorted datasets.

```ecl
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```ecl
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```ecl
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```ecl
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```ecl
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```ecl
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```ecl
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```ecl
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```ecl
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
